Title: Beyond Localization: Recoverable Headroom and Residual Frontier in Repository-Level RAG-APR
Abstract: Repository-level automated program repair (APR) increasingly treats stronger localization as the main path to better repair. We ask a more targeted question: once localization is strengthened, which post-localization levers still provide recoverable gains, which are bounded within our protocol, and what residual frontier remains? We study this question on SWE-bench Lite with three representative repository-level RAG-APR paradigms, Agentless, KGCompass, and ExpeRepair. Our protocol combines Oracl
